name: advance-epics
title: Advance Epics
purpose: Capability: Epic entity and workflow contracts for ADV initiative planning. Epics are durable containers that group related ADV changes and lightweight shell entries, replacing project-level ROADMAP.md as the primary ADV planning surface for initiative-level work while keeping Epic membership optional, order advisory, retrofit-capable, and product-scope aware.

requirements.title: Epic Record Is a Typed, Structural Initiative Container
requirements.body: An Epic MUST be represented by a typed record containing a stable ID, title, narrative context, ordered roadmap entries, a compact status/progress summary, creation/update timestamps, and optional repo/product scope metadata. Correctness-critical Epic state MUST be structural and typed, not prose-only roadmap text. The Epic record MUST NOT depend on project-level ROADMAP.md for its existence or validation.

scenarios.title: Valid Epic record parses successfully
given: A caller provides an Epic record with all required typed fields
scenarios.when: The record is validated against the Epic schema
then: Validation succeeds
Title, narrative, entries, progress summary, and timestamps are preserved

scenarios.title: Epic record rejects missing title
given: An Epic record is missing the title field
scenarios.when: The record is validated against the Epic schema
then: Validation fails with a clear error

requirements.title: Epic Roadmap Supports Linked Changes and Shell Entries
requirements.body: An Epic's ordered entries MUST support exactly two kinds: `change` entries that reference one ADV change and `shell` entries that represent future work. Change entries MUST remain backward-compatible with legacy same-project `change_id` rows and MUST support a project-aware `change_ref` carrying change ID, project ID, optional repo ID, and optional target path. Every shell entry MUST have a title and a rough success/AC hint. Shell entries MUST NOT be required to complete full ADV proposal/discovery before they can exist or be promoted. A change entry MAY carry promotion provenance when it originated from a shell.

scenarios.title: Shell entry requires title and success hint
given: An Epic entry with kind 'shell' is provided
scenarios.when: The entry is validated
then: Validation succeeds when title and success_hint are present
Validation fails when either field is missing

scenarios.title: Change entry requires change ID
given: An Epic entry with kind 'change' is provided
scenarios.when: The entry is validated
then: Validation succeeds when legacy change_id is present
Validation succeeds when change_ref includes change_id and project_id
Validation fails when neither change_id nor change_ref is present

scenarios.title: Project-aware change entry carries audit fields
given: An Epic change entry uses change_ref
scenarios.when: The entry is validated
then: Validation requires title, membership_status, linked_at, linked_by, and link_evidence
Validation preserves repo/project identity

requirements.title: Epic Creation Command Is Goal-First and Overlap-Aware
requirements.body: The `/adv-epic` command MUST guide users through creating or updating Epics with an explicit ultimate goal before mutation. Before creating a new Epic, the command MUST scan related open work through typed Epic/change/backlog reads and surface plausible overlap neutrally. If plausible overlap exists, the command MUST ask the user to choose update/clarify existing, create new, or stop before calling `adv_epic_create`. Initial shell or change entries MUST remain optional and any Epic mutation MUST use typed Epic tools rather than direct ADV state edits.

scenarios.title: Command requires ultimate goal before creation
given: A user invokes `/adv-epic` to create a new Epic
scenarios.when: The command prepares an Epic creation plan
then: The plan includes an explicit ultimate goal
The command obtains final user confirmation before mutation
The Epic narrative preserves the confirmed goal

scenarios.title: Overlap requires user choice before duplicate creation
given: The related-work scan finds one or more plausible overlapping open Epics
scenarios.when: The command presents the overlap evidence
then: The evidence is presented neutrally
The user is asked to choose update/clarify existing, create new, or stop
The command does not call `adv_epic_create` for a plausible duplicate until the user chooses create new

scenarios.title: Initial entries remain optional
given: A user has confirmed an Epic title, ultimate goal, and narrative but has no initial child work
scenarios.when: The command creates the Epic
then: The Epic may be created with zero initial roadmap entries
Shell and linked-change additions remain available through typed Epic tools

requirements.title: Epic Coordination Command Is Read-First and Approval-Gated
requirements.body: The `/adv-coordinate` command MUST inventory active Epics through typed reads before proposing durable mutations. It MUST report ownership-boundary, narrative accuracy, cross-Epic dependency, advisory sequencing, capstone placement, and membership-health findings while distinguishing evidence-backed facts from judgment calls. Epic order findings MUST remain advisory and MUST NOT block gates, tasks, promotion, or change progress. Narrative updates, reorders, and membership repairs MUST use typed Epic tools and require explicit user approval before mutation. The command MUST NOT introduce mandatory Epic membership, auto-enrollment, Jira-like planning primitives, direct ADV state edits, or CLI mutation verbs.

scenarios.title: Coordination report reads before mutation
given: One or more active Epics exist
scenarios.when: `/adv-coordinate` prepares a coordination report
then: Epics are inventoried through typed Epic reads before any mutation is proposed
The report covers alignment, narrative accuracy, dependencies, sequencing, capstone placement, and membership health
Evidence-backed facts are separated from judgment calls

scenarios.title: Order findings remain advisory
given: A sequencing inversion is detected
scenarios.when: The coordination report recommends a reorder
then: The reorder is presented as advisory
No gate, task, promotion, or change progress is blocked solely due to Epic order

scenarios.title: Durable actions require approval and typed tools
given: Narrative edits, reorders, or membership repairs are proposed
scenarios.when: The user has not explicitly approved the proposed durable action
then: No Epic mutation tool is executed
Approved mutations use typed Epic tools such as adv_epic_update, adv_epic_reorder, or adv_epic_repair_membership
Required expected_version and audit evidence fields are preserved

scenarios.title: Coordination adds no new planning primitive
given: The `/adv-coordinate` command is implemented
scenarios.when: Command assets and CLI surfaces are inspected
then: Epic membership remains optional and no change is auto-enrolled
Jira-like assignments, estimates, sprints, boards, and ownership workflows are absent
No CLI mutation verb is introduced for coordination
The command does not instruct agents to directly read or edit ADV state files

requirements.title: Shell Promotion Replaces the Shell Row with Exactly One Linked Change
requirements.body: Promoting a shell entry MUST create or link exactly one ADV change, then replace the shell row in the Epic with a `change` row. The new change row MUST carry promotion provenance that preserves the original shell title and success hint. Promotion MUST be idempotent: retrying promotion of the same shell MUST return the already-linked change without creating duplicate rows.

scenarios.title: Promotion replaces shell with linked change row
given: An Epic contains a shell entry
scenarios.when: The shell is promoted
then: The shell row is removed from the Epic entries
Exactly one 'change' row appears in its place
The change row carries the shell's title and success hint as promotion provenance

scenarios.title: Duplicate promotion returns existing change
given: A shell has already been promoted to a linked change
scenarios.when: Promotion is retried for the same shell entry ID
then: No new change is created
No additional Epic row is added
The existing linked change ID is returned

requirements.title: Epic Order Is Advisory and Must Not Hard-Block Later Entries
requirements.body: Epic entry order MUST affect display and next-work recommendations only. Starting or resuming a later Epic entry MAY warn about earlier incomplete entries, but MUST NOT block gates, tasks, or promotion solely because of order. The advisory-order contract MUST be preserved in v1.

scenarios.title: Later entry can be started while earlier entry is incomplete
given: An Epic has two entries where the first is incomplete
scenarios.when: An agent starts work on the second entry
then: The start or resume succeeds
A warning about earlier incomplete work MAY be surfaced
No hard gate or task blockage is introduced solely due to order

requirements.title: Default Epic View Surfaces Compact Terminal History
requirements.body: The default Epic view MUST show next active and future work prominently and MUST include compact rows for archived or closed child changes. Full historical detail MUST remain available through an explicit view or context fetch, not inflate the default hot-path response.

scenarios.title: Default view distinguishes active and terminal entries
given: An Epic has active, future, archived, and closed entries
scenarios.when: The default Epic view is rendered
then: Active and future entries are clearly visible
Archived/closed entries are shown as compact history
Response size and detail remain bounded

requirements.title: Change Surfaces Show Compact Epic Membership Context
requirements.body: When a change belongs to an Epic, change show/status/resume surfaces MUST surface compact Epic membership context including Epic ID, entry ID, order, title, linked timestamp, owner Epic project ID when known, repo ID when known, and projection source when known. The context MUST be additive and MUST NOT replace or obscure the change's own gates, tasks, or artifacts. Changes without Epic membership MUST render identically to the pre-Epic flow.

scenarios.title: Epic child change shows compact context
given: A change has an epic_membership projection
scenarios.when: The change is shown or resumed
then: Epic ID, entry ID, order, title, and extended project/repo metadata are surfaced when present
The change's own status and tasks remain primary

scenarios.title: Non-Epic changes remain unchanged
given: A change has no epic_membership
scenarios.when: The change is shown or resumed
then: No Epic context is rendered
Existing fields and behavior are unchanged

requirements.title: ADV Next-Work Selection Can Operate from Epics
requirements.body: ADV next-work selection and planning surfaces MUST be able to use Epics as a source of recommended next work without requiring a project-level ROADMAP.md. The recommendation MUST respect Epic order as advisory and MUST surface warnings rather than block when earlier entries are incomplete.

scenarios.title: Next work can be chosen from an Epic
given: An Epic has active and future entries
scenarios.when: ADV next-work selection queries available work
then: Epic entries are considered as candidates
A recommended next entry is surfaced without requiring ROADMAP.md

requirements.title: Epic Membership Remains Optional and Non-Epic Changes Stay Valid
requirements.body: Epic membership MUST be optional for all ADV changes. Existing non-Epic changes, changes created outside Epics, and changes whose Epic is later archived MUST remain valid and continue through the normal gate/task flow. The change schema MUST treat epic_membership as an optional additive projection.

scenarios.title: Legacy change without Epic membership parses cleanly
given: A change record created before Epics exists
scenarios.when: The change is parsed by the current schema
then: Validation succeeds
epic_membership is undefined
All existing fields are preserved

scenarios.title: Malformed epic_membership is rejected
given: A change record contains an epic_membership object missing required fields
scenarios.when: The change is parsed
then: Validation fails with a clear schema error

requirements.title: One Epic Per Change in V1
requirements.body: In v1, each ADV change MAY belong to zero or one Epic. The change schema MUST represent epic_membership as a single optional object, not an array. Product/multi-project Epics MUST still preserve this one-Epic-per-change invariant by storing one compact membership projection on each linked child change.

scenarios.title: Single Epic membership is valid
given: A change record has one epic_membership object
scenarios.when: The change is parsed
then: Validation succeeds
epic_id is accessible

requirements.title: Retroactive Membership Is Audited, Movable, and Repairable
requirements.body: Existing ADV changes MAY be linked into an Epic after creation, unlinked, moved between Epics, removed from a stale parent entry, or retargeted from a stale parent entry through typed tools only. Link, unlink, move, and repair operations MUST require audit evidence, MUST preserve `fast_follow_of` as creation lineage, and MUST update the child change's compact `epic_membership` projection when the child project is reachable. Cross-project membership mutations MUST follow target-path trust rules. Partial failures MUST surface deterministic membership status such as `projection_pending`, `projection_stale`, or `target_unreachable`, with explicit repair paths for projection sync, parent-only removal, and audited retarget.

scenarios.title: Existing change links with child projection
given: An existing ADV change has no Epic membership
scenarios.when: The change is linked to an Epic with audit evidence
then: The Epic records a project-aware change entry
The child change receives exactly one `epic_membership` projection
`fast_follow_of` is not created or changed

scenarios.title: Move preserves one-Epic invariant
given: A child change belongs to one Epic
scenarios.when: The change is moved to another Epic with audit evidence
then: The source Epic no longer appears as the active membership
The destination Epic owns the child projection
No second simultaneous Epic membership is created

scenarios.title: Unreachable target remains recoverable
given: An Epic entry references a target project that cannot be reached
scenarios.when: Membership is shown or repaired
then: The Epic remains readable
The member reports `target_unreachable` or another typed repair status
A repair operation can dry-run before mutating state

scenarios.title: Missing child entry can be removed from parent only
given: An Epic entry references a missing child workflow
scenarios.when: An operator runs audited parent-only stale-entry removal
then: The parent Epic entry is removed through a typed Epic mutation
The missing child workflow is not loaded or mutated
Blank or omitted audit evidence is rejected

scenarios.title: Missing child entry can be retargeted to reachable child
given: An Epic parent entry references missing child A
Reachable child B is supplied with audit evidence
scenarios.when: An operator runs audited stale-entry retarget
then: The parent Epic entry is atomically retargeted to child B
Entry ID, order, and title are preserved where possible
Child B receives or refreshes the compact `epic_membership` projection when reachable

scenarios.title: Retarget refuses conflicting child membership
given: The proposed target child already belongs to a different Epic or entry
scenarios.when: An operator attempts stale-entry retarget
then: The repair returns a typed membership mismatch
The parent Epic entry is not mutated
No second simultaneous Epic membership is created

requirements.title: Epic Product Scope Is Derived from Typed Scope Breadth
requirements.body: Every Epic MUST be product-capable through typed scope metadata. User-facing local/product-spanning classification MUST be derived from the Epic scope repo/project list: one scoped repo renders as local/repo-scoped, multiple scoped repos/projects render as product-spanning, and missing scope renders as legacy/unscoped. `epic_scope.kind` MAY be preserved for backward compatibility but MUST NOT be the correctness authority for classification.

scenarios.title: Single-repo scope renders local
given: An Epic scope contains exactly one repo entry
The legacy kind field says `product`
scenarios.when: The Epic is rendered or classified
then: The derived scope label is local or repo-scoped
The legacy kind field is not used as the classification authority

scenarios.title: Multi-repo scope renders product-spanning
given: An Epic scope contains two repo/project entries
scenarios.when: The Epic is rendered or classified
then: The derived scope label is product-spanning
Owner project and repo_project_id metadata are preserved

scenarios.title: Missing scope remains legacy-readable
given: A legacy Epic has no epic_scope
scenarios.when: The Epic is parsed or shown
then: Validation succeeds
The derived scope label is legacy/unscoped
The Epic can be backfilled through typed scope mutation

requirements.title: Existing Active Epics Support Audited Scope Mutation
requirements.body: An active Epic's typed scope metadata MUST be mutable after creation through a typed, audited, optimistic-concurrency mutation path. Scope mutation MUST require audit evidence and an expected version, MUST reject stale writes without changing scope, and MUST NOT silently orphan linked child-change entries when repos/projects are removed. Dry-run preview SHOULD report the derived scope label and removal impact before mutation.

scenarios.title: Active Epic expands scope
given: An active Epic has one scoped repo
The caller supplies a current expected version and audit evidence
scenarios.when: A typed scope update adds a second repo/project entry
then: The Epic scope contains both repos
The Epic version increments
The derived scope label becomes product-spanning

scenarios.title: Stale scope update is rejected
given: An active Epic has version 3
scenarios.when: A scope update is submitted with expected version 2
then: A typed stale-version conflict is returned
The existing scope is unchanged

scenarios.title: Scope removal cannot orphan children silently
given: An Epic has a linked child entry for repo A
scenarios.when: A scope update removes repo A
then: The update is rejected, requires explicit disposition, or surfaces typed repair status
No child epic_membership projection is silently detached

requirements.title: Active Duplicate Epics Can Merge into One Survivor
requirements.body: Active duplicate Epics MAY be merged into one survivor Epic through a typed, audited, plan-first mutation path. Merge planning MUST surface unique entries, conflicts, target-project confirmations, and terminal-source rejection before mutation. Execution MUST preserve the one-owning-Epic-per-change invariant, MUST update reachable child epic_membership projections to the survivor, MUST require explicit conflict disposition instead of heuristic dedupe, and MUST leave the source Epic readable with a survivor pointer and no active next-work. Completed, archived, or already-merged source Epics MUST NOT be merged into active survivors; they may be referenced as historical context only.

scenarios.title: Unique entries move to survivor
given: Two active Epics contain unique linked changes
The caller supplies audit evidence and current versions
scenarios.when: The source Epic is merged into the survivor
then: Unique entries appear on the survivor
Reachable child epic_membership projections point to the survivor
No child change has two owning Epic memberships

scenarios.title: Conflicting entries require disposition
given: The source and survivor Epics contain similar shell entries or duplicate child changes
scenarios.when: A merge is executed without conflict resolution
then: The merge is rejected with typed conflict details
No heuristic title-based dedupe is applied
No source entries are silently dropped

scenarios.title: Merged source remains readable but inactive
given: A source Epic has been successfully merged into a survivor
scenarios.when: The source Epic is shown or considered for next work
then: The source Epic exposes a merged_into survivor pointer with audit evidence
The source Epic has no active next-work recommendation
The source Epic remains queryable for history

scenarios.title: Terminal sources are historical references only
given: A source Epic is completed, archived, or already merged
scenarios.when: A caller attempts to merge it into an active survivor
then: The merge mutation is rejected
The source may be referenced as historical context only

requirements.title: Archive Synchronizes Epic Child Terminal State
requirements.body: When an ADV change with `epic_membership` is archived, the archive/release flow MUST load the parent Epic, project the child change's terminal state onto the linked Epic entry after release proof, verify the terminal Epic entry/projection state, and surface Epic evidence in the archive report. Already-archived child changes whose Epic entries remain active MUST be repairable/backfillable from canonical child/archive state through typed Epic tools such as `adv_epic_repair_membership`; direct ADV state edits are forbidden. Changes without Epic membership MUST continue through the normal archive flow. Epic order MUST remain advisory and MUST NOT block archive solely because earlier entries are incomplete.

scenarios.title: Epic child archive records terminal summary
given: A change has an `epic_membership` projection
The archive flow has obtained valid release proof for the child change
scenarios.when: The change is marked archived
then: The parent Epic entry receives a terminal summary or equivalent typed terminal-history state
The archived child appears in compact Epic history rather than active next work
The archive report includes Epic verification evidence

scenarios.title: Stale projection uses typed repair path
given: An archived child change has an Epic entry whose membership projection is stale, pending, or target-unreachable
scenarios.when: Archive verification inspects the parent Epic
then: The flow uses `adv_epic_repair_membership` or another typed Epic tool to repair or mark the projection
No direct ADV state files are read or edited
Unresolved repair status is surfaced in archive evidence

scenarios.title: Retroactive repair backfills archived child progress
given: An Epic entry remains active even though the linked child change is already `archived` or `closed`
scenarios.when: `adv_epic_repair_membership mode=sync_child_projection` runs with canonical child/archive evidence
then: The existing Epic entry receives terminal summary state
Epic progress recomputes completed entries and next entry from terminal summaries
The repair result reports terminal projection evidence

scenarios.title: Non-Epic archive remains unchanged
given: A change has no `epic_membership` projection
scenarios.when: The change is archived
then: No Epic lookup or mutation is required
The normal archive proof and report behavior remain valid
The report may record `Epic: n/a`

scenarios.title: Earlier incomplete Epic entries do not block archive by order
given: A child change belongs to an Epic
An earlier Epic entry is incomplete
scenarios.when: The child change archive flow verifies Epic context
then: The earlier incomplete entry may be reported as advisory context
The archive is not blocked solely by Epic order
Phase 9 release proof remains the archive authority

requirements.title: Product Epics Carry Repo and Project Scope Metadata
requirements.body: An Epic MAY be scoped to a single repo or to a product spanning multiple repositories/projects. Product Epic scope metadata MUST include the owner ADV project ID and a typed repo list containing repo ID, repo project ID, role, required flag, and optional path. Product Epics MUST NOT require duplicate repo-local Epics for one initiative.

scenarios.title: Product scope spans multiple repos
given: An Epic scope includes two configured repositories
scenarios.when: The Epic is validated
then: Validation succeeds
Owner project ID and each repo_project_id are preserved
No duplicate repo-local Epic is required

scenarios.title: Scope repo requires project identity
given: An Epic scope repo is missing repo_project_id
scenarios.when: The scope is validated
then: Validation fails with a clear schema error

requirements.title: V1 Does Not Clone Project-Management Workflows
requirements.body: Advance Epics v1 MUST NOT add Jira-like assignments, estimates, boards, sprints, ownership workflows, or clone GitHub Projects. Epic ordering, next-work recommendation, and compact context are the only planning primitives added in v1.

scenarios.title: Epic schema rejects project-management fields
given: A caller attempts to add assignee, estimate, sprint, or board fields to an Epic record
scenarios.when: The record is validated
then: Those fields are not recognized as required Epic structure

requirements.title: Missing Epic and Stale Links Produce Deterministic Errors or Recoverable Warnings
requirements.body: Operations referencing a missing Epic, a stale child change link, a duplicate promotion, or a concurrent reorder/promotion MUST produce deterministic errors or recoverable warnings. Failure modes MUST be typed so callers can distinguish not-found, conflict, and stale-state conditions and retry or reconcile safely.

scenarios.title: Missing Epic returns not-found error
given: A caller requests an Epic that does not exist
scenarios.when: The lookup is performed
then: A typed not-found error is returned

scenarios.title: Concurrent edit yields stale-version warning or conflict
given: Two callers mutate the same Epic concurrently
scenarios.when: The second mutation is applied against a stale version
then: A typed conflict or stale-state response is returned

requirements.title: Epic List CLI Is Read-Only, Worker-Free, and Fail-Closed
requirements.body: The `adv epic list --json` CLI command MUST return live Temporal-backed Epic IDs for the current project as stable JSON. The command MUST use Temporal Visibility enumeration of `epicWorkflow` executions and project-scoped workflow ID prefix filtering, MUST NOT read ADV external state files, MUST NOT query or hydrate each Epic workflow, MUST NOT require a worker polling the project task queue, and MUST fail closed with structured JSON error metadata instead of silently returning stale disk data. The Epic CLI namespace MUST remain read-only and MUST NOT expose mutation subcommands.

scenarios.title: Epic list returns live JSON IDs
given: A git-backed project has Epic workflows visible to Temporal
scenarios.when: `adv epic list --json` runs from the project
then: The command exits 0
The JSON payload includes `source: "temporal"`, `live: true`, `stale: false`, `generated_at`, `project_id`, and `epics`
Each Epic entry is an object containing at least `id`

scenarios.title: Epic list filters by current project prefix
given: Temporal Visibility returns Epic workflow IDs for multiple ADV projects
scenarios.when: The Epic list CLI builds its payload
then: Only workflow IDs under `adv/epic/{projectId}/` appear in `epics`
Workflow IDs outside the current project prefix are excluded
Empty Epic ID suffixes are ignored

scenarios.title: Epic list fails closed on unavailable live state
given: Temporal connection or Visibility listing fails, or the command is run outside a git project
scenarios.when: `adv epic list --json` handles the failure
then: The command exits non-zero
The JSON payload includes `source: "temporal"`, `live: false`, `stale: false`, `epics: []`, `error`, and `remediation`
No disk-projected Epic rows are returned as a fallback

scenarios.title: Epic CLI namespace remains read-only
given: A caller invokes the `adv epic` CLI namespace
scenarios.when: The CLI dispatches the nested command
then: Only the read-only `list` operation is accepted
Mutation verbs such as `create`, `update`, `delete`, and `archive` are not dispatched
The list path does not query per-Epic workflow state

requirements.title: Epic Visibility Index Respects Temporal Search-Attribute Constraints
requirements.body: The child-change Visibility index for Epic lookup MUST use a single-value `Keyword` search attribute, following the pattern of `AdvBacklogIssueNumber`, and MUST NOT exceed the existing custom `KeywordList` cap. Epic ID on child changes MUST be derived from the `epic_membership` projection.

scenarios.title: Epic ID is indexed as single Keyword
given: A child change has epic_membership.epic_id
scenarios.when: Search attributes are built
then: AdvEpicId is set as a single-value Keyword
No KeywordList attribute is used for Epic membership
